blocks.content: The Thanksgiving phone call started at seven her time, eight his. It lasted until almost two in the morning.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Describe your Thanksgiving plate.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Darius's mom sent a whole box of food. Turkey, stuffing, sweet potato — she didn't want him eating dining hall Thanksgiving.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She sounds wonderful.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She's amazing. She sent enough for both of us. We watched a game and ate until we couldn't move and honestly it was fine. Better than I expected.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: My mom made too much as usual. We have enough left over to last until Christmas.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Save me the pie.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: The pie will not last until December.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Tragic.

They talked about everything and nothing — her grandmother's story about a Thanksgiving in 1967 that had apparently involved a small fire, Kit's film rough cut coming together finally, what Eli had texted the group (extensively, about Priya), what it felt like to come back to Millhaven and find it the same.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Does it feel different to you. When you think about going back.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Different how.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Like — does home mean the same thing it did in August.
He thought about it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Millhaven's still home. But something shifted. Like I know it's mine in a different way now that I've been somewhere else.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah. Exactly that.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Do you like it there. Minnesota. Honestly.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah. I really do. The cold is — a commitment — but the program is everything I wanted. My professor told me this week I should consider graduate school eventually.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Daisy.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: She said it like it was obvious. Like it was already decided.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Because it is obvious.
She was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Kit, I'm really glad we had that fight.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I mean it. It needed to happen. Some of those things needed to be said out loud.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: The friendship thing.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I've been thinking about what you said. About being scared to name it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: And.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: And I want to talk about it properly. Not on the phone. In person.
She pulled her blanket tighter. Outside, Millhaven was quiet the way it always got at night.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: December.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: December.
